metadata.description: Retired LANL employee (retired 2017); disappeared on foot without wallet, keys, phone, or vehicle from Los Alamos, NM.
LANL administrative assistant; last seen walking on NM-518 near Talpa; both phones found factory-reset at home.
JPL research scientist (1998-2022); planetary defense (DART, NEAT, Dawn); died age 59, cause of death undisclosed.
Caltech/IPAC astrophysicist (1997-2026); shot on front porch by suspect Freddy Snyder; suspect arrested and charged.
Director, Materials Processing Group at JPL; co-inventor of Mondaloy superalloy; disappeared on Mt. Waterman Trail hike.
Associate Director, Chemical Biology at Novartis; walked away from home without phone/wallet after parents' deaths; body found in Lake Quannapowitt; no foul play suspected.
JPL principal researcher (~1999-2024); SBG-VSWIR, AMR, HIFI/Herschel instruments; died age 61, cause undisclosed.
Alleged KCNSC contractor (single anonymous source); left Albuquerque home on foot carrying a handgun, no phone/wallet/keys.
MIT Herman Feshbach Professor of Physics; Director, MIT Plasma Science and Fusion Center; shot by former classmate Claudio Valente.
Co-founder, Institute for Exotic Science; gravity-modification researcher; death ruled suicide; father rejects conspiracy.
Retired USAF two-star general; former AFRL commander and SAPOC executive secretary; disappeared from Albuquerque home with wallet and revolver but without phone or glasses.
DOE/NNSA nuclear weapons research lab in Los Alamos, NM. ~14,000 employees.
NASA center managed by Caltech in Pasadena, CA. ~6,000 employees. Planetary science, space exploration, Earth observation.
Infrared Processing and Analysis Center at Caltech; operates Spitzer, NEOWISE, NEO Surveyor data systems.

nodes.label: MIT Plasma Science and Fusion Center

metadata.description: MIT research center founded at DOE request; 250+ researchers; SPARC tokamak partnership with Commonwealth Fusion Systems.
NNSA facility managed by Honeywell FM&T; produces non-nuclear components for U.S. nuclear weapons. Albuquerque satellite facility.
USAF primary S&T organization at Wright-Patterson AFB; ~10,800 personnel; $4B+ annual portfolio.
Novartis pharmaceutical research division. Not defense or government-affiliated.
Major U.S. rocket propulsion manufacturer; AR1 engine, RS-25; now part of L3Harris Technologies.
Defense technology company; directed energy, space warfare, missile defense, cyber, C4ISR.
NASA center in Huntsville, AL; propulsion research. Richard Eskridge (Amy's father) was a retired engineer here.
Home of LANL; Chavez disappeared here.
Casias last seen walking on NM-518 near Talpa.
Garcia and McCasland disappeared from Albuquerque residences. Near Kirtland AFB, Sandia Labs, KCNSC-NM.
Hicks (Sunland), Maiwald (LA), Reza (Angeles NF), Grillmair (Llano) -- all within ~60 miles.
Loureiro shot at his apartment at 9 Gibbs Street.
Thomas walked away from Murray Street home; body found in Lake Quannapowitt.
Eskridge died at home; NASA Marshall Space Flight Center located here.
DOE/NNSA nuclear weapons research, design, and production infrastructure (LANL, KCNSC, Sandia).
Rocket propulsion and advanced metallurgy research domain; includes Mondaloy superalloy, AR1 engine, space-based laser.
NASA planetary missions (DART, NEAT, Dawn, NEOWISE, NEO Surveyor, SBG-VSWIR, Herschel/HIFI).
DOE/ARPA-E funded fusion research; SPARC tokamak; Commonwealth Fusion Systems partnership.
Informal network around UAP/UFO disclosure; DeLonge/To The Stars; Congressional hearings; Wright-Patterson lore.
Classified DOD programs overseen by SAPOC; McCasland served as SAPOC executive secretary.

tight.description: Documented, verifiable overlaps confirmed by primary or credible secondary sources. Includes institutional co-employment (LANL, JPL, Caltech/IPAC), geographic clustering (NM, LA County, Massachusetts), confirmed research domains, and direct person-to-person institutional connections.
tight.rendering_notes: Solid lines, full opacity. These connections are factual. Color by edge_type. Person-to-person institutional edges should be visually prominent.

medium.description: Research-domain adjacencies, institutional funding chains, behavioral parallels (left without belongings), and connections requiring inference beyond documented facts but grounded in reported information. Includes the alleged Garcia-KCNSC link and geographic co-location without documented personal connection.
medium.rendering_notes: Dashed lines, 70% opacity. These connections are inferential but evidence-grounded. Label each with its confidence rating.

corkboard.description: Speculative connections drawn from UAP-community theorizing, social media narratives, foreign intelligence targeting theories, and inferences that go substantially beyond documented evidence. Includes the DeLonge/UAP overlay, Reza-McCasland speculative link via AFRL, Eskridge's self-reported antigravity claims, and Garcia's unverified nuclear-facility connection to targeting narratives.
corkboard.rendering_notes: Dotted lines, 40% opacity, red or orange color. These are included for transparency, not endorsement. Each should carry a visible caveat label.
